papers.title: EmbodimentSemantic: A Spatial Scene-Graph Dataset and Benchmark for Vision-Language Models on Embodied Manipulation Trajectories
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.00020v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Spatial grounding remains a key limitation of vision-language-action (VLA) systems for robotic manipulation. While current models can recognize objects and follow language instructions, they often lack an explicit representation of how objects are arranged in space, including support, containment, ordering, occlusion, and depth-sensitive relations. We introduce EmbodimentSemantic, a spatial scene-graph dataset and benchmark for evaluating relationa
papers.reason: 提出用于具身操作轨迹的空间场景图数据集与基准，旨在解决VLA空间 grounding 缺失问题。属于重要数据资源，但非直接的方法论突破或可立即复用的训练框架。

papers.title: Joint Discovery of Object and Action Symbols through Effect Prediction for Robotic Manipulation Planning
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.00031v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: To perform complex manipulation planning, autonomous robots are required to abstract continuous, high-dimensional sensorimotor interactions into discrete object and action representations. Earlier work either categorized objects based on visual appearances, which fails to distinguish objects that appear similar but behave differently, or based on effects under interaction, but was limited to predefined actions. papers.reason: 通过效应预测联合发现物体与动作符号以辅助规划。方法偏向传统符号主义与规划结合，缺乏明确的VLA架构集成路径及大规模实证对比，暂归为值得了解。

papers.title: Learning Dexterous Manipulation Using Contact Wrench Guidance From Human Demonstration
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.00033v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Dexterous robot manipulation can benefit from the abundance of human demonstrations, but transferring such demonstrations to robot policies remains challenging. We present Contact Wrench Guidance from Human Demonstration in Robotic Dexterous Manipulation (CHORD), a framework for long-horizon manipulation of rigid and articulated objects with reinforcement learning. The key idea is object-centric contact wrench space guidance: we represent human and

papers.title: Unleashing More Actions via Action Compositional Training for VLA Models
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.00351v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-Language-Action models excel at robotic manipulation, driven by the scale and diversity of demonstration data. However, standard training paradigms often cause VLA models to severely overfit to specific behavioral patterns, rendering them unable to generalize to out-of-distribution scenarios even when those scenarios merely require novel combinations of identical sub-skills. papers.title: From Technical Metrics to User Perception: A User Study of a Multimodal Human-Robot Interaction System for Object Detection and Grasping
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.00530v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Improvements in the technical performance of human--robot interaction (HRI) systems do not automatically translate into differences that human users can detect during live interaction. papers.reason: 聚焦人机交互中的用户感知与技术指标差异，属于 HRI 心理学/社会学范畴，无机器人操作算法、VLA 架构或控制层面的技术贡献。

papers.title: Domain Arithmetic: One-Shot VLA Adaptation under Environmental Shifts
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.00666v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models often fail to perform the same learned tasks under environmental shifts, such as changes in camera pose and shifts to a different but similar robot (e.g., from Panda to UR5e). Adapting these models to the shifted environment (i.e., target domain) often requires training on multiple demonstrations for each task, which are costly to collect. papers.title: AutoSpeed: Annotation-Free Stage-Adaptive Motion Speed Learning for Robot Manipulation
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.01051v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Different stages of manipulation tasks exhibit varying levels of difficulty, suggesting stage-dependent motion speeds and temporal prediction horizons. papers.title: Human-Centric Transferable Tactile Pre-Training for Dexterous Robotic Manipulation
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.01067v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As an essential modality for dexterous and contact-rich tasks, tactile sensing provides precise force feedback that cannot be reliably inferred from vision. papers.title: FAR: Failure-Aware Retry for Test-Time Recovery and Continual Policy Improvement
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.01111v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Robot policies inevitably encounter failures when deployed in real environments. papers.title: FurnitureVLA: Learning Long-Horizon Bimanual Furniture Assembly with Vision-Language-Action Model
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.01212v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Current work on robot furniture assembly mostly focuses on toy-scale settings or single-arm manipulation. papers.title: ABot-M0.5: Unified Mobility-and-Manipulation World Action Model

papers.title: VolumeDP: Modeling Volumetric Representation for Manipulation Policy Learning
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2603.17720v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Imitation learning is a prominent paradigm for robotic manipulation. papers.title: PhyPush: One Push is All You Need for Sensorless Physical Property Estimation with Physics-Guided Transformers
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2605.26284v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Accurately estimating object mass and friction is fundamental to reliable robotic manipulation. papers.title: Building a Scalable, Reproducible, Evaluatable, and Closed-Loop Simulation Environment Foundation for Embodied Intelligence

papers.title: Training Vision-Language-Action Models with Dense Embodied Chain-of-Thought Supervision
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2606.30552v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Cross-embodiment transfer in vision-language-action (VLA) models remains challenging because low-level state and action spaces differ fundamentally across robot platforms. papers.title: The Quadruped Soft Tail: Compliant Grasping and Swabbing for Contamination Surveys in Harsh Environments

papers.title: Multisensory Continual Learning: Adapting Pretrained Visuomotor Policies to Force
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2606.30988v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Robot manipulation often relies on sensory feedback beyond vision, particularly in contact-rich settings where force, tactile, or audio signals reveal interaction states that are not directly observable from images. papers.title: 3D HAMSTER: Bridging Planning and Control in Hierarchical Vision Language Action Models through 3D Trajectory Guidance
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2606.31329v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Hierarchical Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models decouple high-level planning from low-level control to improve generalization in robot manipulation. papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2604.16993v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: As embodied AI transitions to real-world deployment, the success of the Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN) task tends to evolve from mere reachability to social compliance. papers.title: VideoSearch-R1: Iterative Video Retrieval and Reasoning via Soft Query Refinement

papers.title: DeWorldSG: Depth-Aware 3D Semantic Scene Graph Generation via World-Model Priors
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.00889v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We present DeWorldSG, a novel framework that generates spatio-temporally robust 3D Semantic Scene Graphs from RGB-D sequences. papers.title: EgoSim: Egocentric World Simulator for Embodied Interaction Generation
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2604.01001v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: We introduce EgoSim, a closed-loop egocentric world simulator that generates spatially consistent interaction videos and persistently updates the underlying 3D scene state for continuous simulation. papers.title: Task-Relevant Representation Decoupling for Visual Reinforcement Learning Generalization
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.00796v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Visual Reinforcement Learning (VRL) has achieved considerable success in solving control tasks. papers.title: Memory-Native Non-Terrestrial Networks for Embodied Intelligence

papers.title: 3D Point World Models: Point Completion Enables More Accurate Dynamics Learning

papers.title: Valdi: Value Diffusion World Models
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.00917v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: World models can enable Model Predictive Control (MPC), but this requires dynamics prediction that is both fast enough for online use and expressive enough to represent uncertain futures. Diffusion models offer a natural mechanism for modeling uncertain dynamics, yet their iterative inference procedure makes them difficult to use for low-latency latent planning. papers.reason: 提出值扩散世界模型以支持 MPC。虽涉及控制，但核心在于世界模型的价值函数建模，未直接对比或集成于主流 VLA 架构，相关性中等。

papers.title: Language-Critique Imitation Learning from Suboptimal Demonstrations
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.01225v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Prior work on imitation learning from suboptimal demonstrations typically relies on compressed supervision signals such as confidence estimates, discriminator scores, or importance weights. These scalar signals are inherently limited, as they cannot explicitly express intermediate reasoning about task progress, failure modes, or corrective actions. We propose a language-critique framework for imitation learning from suboptimal demonstrations that
papers.reason: 利用语言批评从次优演示中学习，提升 IL 数据利用率。方法可直接应用于 VLA 训练以处理低质量数据，具备明确的本周复用路径和实质贡献。

papers.title: UniDrive-WM: Unified Understanding, Planning and Generation World Model for Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2601.04453v4 Announce Type: replace Abstract: World models have become central to autonomous driving, where accurate scene understanding and future prediction are crucial for safe control. Recent work has explored using vision-language models (VLMs) for planning, yet existing approaches typically treat perception, prediction, and planning as separate modules. papers.reason: 专注于自动驾驶的世界模型，虽涉及规划但与机器人机械臂操作场景不同。自动驾驶与具身操作在传感器配置、控制频率及安全约束上存在本质差异。

papers.title: Divide and Conquer: Decoupled Representation Alignment for Multimodal World Models
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2605.01896v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Emerging multi-modal world models attempt to jointly generate videos across diverse modalities (e.g., RGB, depth, and mask), yet they fail to fully exploit the rich priors of existing foundation models. We propose $M^2$-REPA, the first representation alignment method tailored for multi-modal video generation. papers.reason: 提出解耦表示对齐方法以优化多模态世界模型。虽有助于多模态融合，但侧重于生成模型的对齐技巧，未直接展示其在 VLA 策略学习中的有效性。

papers.title: Holo-World: Unified Camera, Object and Weather Control for Video World Model
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2606.20083v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Video world models are moving toward preserving an observed world under controllable camera and object motion while allowing its environmental state to change. Yet these controls remain isolated, and weather generation typically relies on a source video or reconstructed scene that already specifies future structure. papers.reason: 实现视频世界模型中相机、物体和天气的统一控制。属于生成式世界模型的可控性研究，与 VLA 的策略控制目标距离较远，暂归为值得了解。

papers.title: MemoBench: Benchmarking World Modeling in Dynamically Changing Environments
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2606.27537v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Video generation models aspire to simulate dynamic environments, and several benchmarks now evaluate memory consistency across frames. However, most assess consistency only while the target remains in view, and the few that force objects out of view evaluate static scenes where nothing changes during occlusion. papers.reason: 提出动态环境下的世界模型记忆一致性基准。属于评估工具，虽重要但非算法创新，需等待基于此基准的 VLA 方法出现。

papers.title: Generative Modeling of Quantum Distribution with Functional Flow Matching
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.00301v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The emergence of powerful deep generative models based on diffusion and flow matching has enabled the learning and modeling of complex distributions. Learning quantum distributions, however, remains challenging due to the inherent difficulty of accurately modeling the meaningful physical properties of quantum states. papers.reason: 量子分布的生成建模，属于量子计算与物理交叉领域。无机器人操作场景，与 VLA 完全无关。

papers.title: MetaOthello: A Controlled Study of Multiple World Models in Transformers
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2602.23164v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Foundation models must handle multiple generative processes, yet mechanistic interpretability largely studies capabilities in isolation; it remains unclear how a single transformer organizes multiple, potentially conflicting "world models". Previous experiments on Othello playing neural-networks test world-model learning but focus on a single game with a single set of rules. papers.reason: 在 Transformer 中研究多个世界模型的机制解释。属于机理可解释性研究，虽深刻但非直接的 VLA 控制算法创新，适合理论研究而非工程应用。
